text.text:
Hi! I wanted to share my positive experience with one of your locations and associates. Ciara Moore delivered an exceptional experience during my visit to Chrome Hearts in Beverly Hills today. From the moment I walked in, she was welcoming, attentive, and incredibly patient as I took my time exploring different pieces.

What stood out most was her product knowledge and genuine approach—she didn’t rush the process or pressure me, but instead made thoughtful suggestions and ensured I felt confident in my decisions. That level of service is rare and truly elevates the entire brand experience.

As someone who understands the importance of high-level customer service, I can confidently say Ciara represents the brand in the best way possible. I made a purchase today, and I am looking forward to working with her again.

text.text:
Kenny is hands down my favorite sales associate here - he's truly outstanding at his job, and every shopping experience I've had at this store has been an absolute blast. I want to give a big shout-out to Kenny for consistently keeping it real since day one. He never exerts any pressure on me to make purchases, and that's something I truly appreciate.

Now, I can understand why some people might leave negative feedback about this place. This particular store can be a bit tricky to access if you're looking to shop in person. They insist on appointments for in-store shopping, and the location itself is tucked away, with a gated entrance. But there's a method to Chrome Hearts' madness. The management's intention is clear - they want to provide a personalized 1:1 shopping experience for everyone who visits the store.

In other words, they have exclusive merchandise, and their goal is to cater to people who genuinely appreciate the brand. Moreover, the weekly restocks are a pleasant surprise, with different items on offer each time. So, when you make a visit to this location, it's almost like testing your luck and discovering something new every time.

text.text:
HIGHLY DISAPPOINTING experience at this location. I had an appointment and was let inside, but an associate was extremely unhelpful and not interested in assisting at all. Zero effort to show products and it felt like being rushed out.

Was genuinely interested in making a purchase, and at one point, associate goes “that’s literally the first thing I said,”. Unbelievable ego

For a brand with this reputation, you would not expect this abysmal excuse of customer service.

text.text: Been going go this location for the last decade and never had a problem with security before…until December 10th around 4pm I had the pleasure of speaking to the most condescending security of all time….most awful tone , acting like he was guarding the nuclear codes…checked my id?? never happened to me at a ch store before lol just for us to wait 20 minutes and the store is sold out of everything…not the stores fault. But that security guard needs to go. I never write reviews, he was just that awful and rude.

But that security guard needs to go. I never write reviews, he was just that awful and rude.

text.text: My experience with Chrome Hearts LA with visit of  10 CH boutiques before - this one is the worst. I was able to tell it only by one person. Imagine the situation, you are the person who got an appointment at this store, you are leaving the store and in front of you gate to leave the territory. You are being followed by a security guy and you come to the gate and try to open it, security guy just watches you trying to open it and does nothing and tells nothing. Waits for you to stop trying, shows you with a hand to move back and allow him to pass without saying a word, pulls out a key from a pocket, opens the gate, lets you out, instantly closes it in front of the next customer coming in and without hello, what I can do for you, how can I help, just asks What?
